title: Beyond Good and Evil - Section 225 - Discipline of Suffering
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Do not frame suffering as good in itself. Use only to explain Nietzsche’s idea that total avoidance of hardship blocks growth.
misreading_risk: Can be oversimplified into macho pain-worship.
raw_text:
Nietzsche argues that the elevation of humanity has often involved struggle, tension, hardship, and the discipline of suffering. His point is not that pain is sacred or that suffering should be pursued for its own sake. The point is that a life organized entirely around avoiding difficulty is unlikely to produce strength, depth, or self-overcoming.

For this project, the section is best used when the user treats all hardship as meaningless or believes growth should come without friction or cost.
